label: Procedural memory & skills

node.label: Procedural memory & skills
node.definition: Reusable procedures, policies, tools, and routines encode how to perform tasks.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Procedural memory & skills is to connect Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations, Differentiable memory read/write to downstream capabilities including Skill libraries, workflows & procedural plans.
examples: substrate for Skill libraries, workflows & procedural plans
combined with Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations
limitations: Stored or retrieved information can be stale, private, contradictory, inaccessible, or incorrectly ranked at the moment it is needed. Its direct dependencies include Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations, Differentiable memory read/write.

dependencies.rationale: Procedural memory & skills stores, selects, or obtains information through Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations.
Procedural memory & skills uses Differentiable memory read/write as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Procedural memory & skills uses Continual & online learning as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
